title: तैत्तिरीय उपनिषद् — शिक्षावल्ली
devanagariText:
॥ तैत्तिरीय उपनिषद् — शिक्षावल्ली ॥

ॐ शं नो मित्रः शं वरुणः। शं नो भवत्वर्यमा।
शं न इन्द्रो बृहस्पतिः। शं नो विष्णुरुरुक्रमः।
नमो ब्रह्मणे। नमस्ते वायो।
त्वमेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्मासि। त्वामेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्म वदिष्यामि।
ऋतं वदिष्यामि। सत्यं वदिष्यामि।
तन्मामवतु। तद्वक्तारमवतु।
अवतु माम्। अवतु वक्तारम्।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ प्रथमोऽनुवाकः — शिक्षा ॥

शीक्षां व्याख्यास्यामः। वर्णः स्वरः। मात्रा बलम्।
साम सन्तानः। इत्युक्तः शीक्षाध्यायः॥ १॥

॥ द्वितीयोऽनुवाकः — संहिता ॥

ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु। मा विद्विषावहै।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

संहिता उपनिषद्। पूर्वरूपम्। उत्तररूपम्। सन्धिः। सन्धानम्।
इत्यधिशीक्षम्। अथाध्यात्मम्। पूर्वरूपम्। उत्तररूपम्।
आकाशः सन्धिः। प्राणः सन्धानम्। इत्यधिप्रजम्॥ २॥

॥ तृतीयोऽनुवाकः — मेधा ॥

सह नौ यशः। सह नौ ब्रह्मवर्चसम्।
मेधां देवी जुषमाणा न आगात्।
ब्रह्मवर्चसं महः। यशो भगो अमृतत्वमागात्॥ ३॥

॥ चतुर्थोऽनुवाकः — व्याहृतिः ॥

भूर्भुवः सुवः इति वा एतास्तिस्रो व्याहृतयः।
तासामु ह स्मैतां चतुर्थीं माहाचमस्यः प्रवेदयते। मह इति।
तद्ब्रह्म। स आत्मा। अङ्गान्यन्या देवताः।
भूरिति वा अयं लोकः। भुव इत्यन्तरिक्षम्।
सुवरित्यसौ लोकः। मह इत्यादित्यः॥ ४॥

॥ एकादशोऽनुवाकः — उपदेश ॥

सत्यं वद। धर्मं चर। स्वाध्यायान्मा प्रमदः।
आचार्याय प्रियं धनमाहृत्य प्रजातन्तुं मा व्यवच्छेत्सीः।
सत्यान्न प्रमदितव्यम्। धर्मान्न प्रमदितव्यम्।
कुशलान्न प्रमदितव्यम्। भूत्यै न प्रमदितव्यम्।
स्वाध्यायप्रवचनाभ्यां न प्रमदितव्यम्॥

देवपितृकार्याभ्यां न प्रमदितव्यम्।
मातृदेवो भव। पितृदेवो भव।
आचार्यदेवो भव। अतिथिदेवो भव।
यान्यनवद्यानि कर्माणि तानि सेवितव्यानि। नो इतराणि।
यान्यस्माकं सुचरितानि तानि त्वयोपास्यानि। नो इतराणि॥

श्रद्धया देयम्। अश्रद्धया अदेयम्।
श्रिया देयम्। ह्रिया देयम्। भिया देयम्। संविदा देयम्।
एष आदेशः। एष उपदेशः।
एषा वेदोपनिषत्। एतदनुशासनम्।
एवमुपासितव्यम्। एवमु चैतदुपास्यम्॥ ११॥

॥ द्वादशोऽनुवाकः ॥

ॐ शं नो मित्रः शं वरुणः। शं नो भवत्वर्यमा।
शं न इन्द्रो बृहस्पतिः। शं नो विष्णुरुरुक्रमः।
नमो ब्रह्मणे। नमस्ते वायो।
त्वमेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्मासि। त्वमेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्म वदिष्यामि।
ऋतमवादिषम्। सत्यमवादिषम्।
तन्मामावीत्। तद्वक्तारमावीत्।
आवीन्माम्। आवीद्वक्तारम्।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥ १२॥

॥ इति तैत्तिरीय उपनिषद् शिक्षावल्ली सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Taittiriya Upanishad — Shiksha Valli ||

Om Sham no Mitrah sham Varunah | Sham no bhavatv-Aryama |
Sham na Indro Brihaspatih | Sham no Vishnur-urukramah |
Namo Brahmane | Namaste Vayo |
Tvam-eva pratyaksham Brahma-asi | Tvam-eva pratyaksham Brahma vadishyami |
Ritam vadishyami | Satyam vadishyami |
Tan-mam-avatu | Tad-vaktaram-avatu |
Avatu mam | Avatu vaktaram |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

|| Prathamo-anuvakah — Shiksha ||

Shiksham vyakhyasyamah | Varnah svarah | Matra balam |
Sama santanah | Ity-uktah Shiksha-adhyayah || 1 ||

|| Dvitiyo-anuvakah — Samhita ||

Om Saha nav-avatu | Saha nau bhunaktu |
Saha viryam karavavhai |
Tejasvi nav-adhitam-astu | Ma vidvishavahai |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

Samhita Upanishad | Purva-rupam | Uttara-rupam | Sandhih | Sandhanam |
Ity-adhi-shiksham | Atha-adhyatmam | Purva-rupam | Uttara-rupam |
Akaashah sandhih | Pranah sandhanam | Ity-adhiprajam || 2 ||

|| Tritiyo-anuvakah — Medha ||

Saha nau yashah | Saha nau Brahma-varchasam |
Medham devi jushamanaa na aagaat |
Brahma-varchasam mahah | Yasho bhago amritatvam-aagaat || 3 ||

|| Chaturtho-anuvakah — Vyahriti ||

Bhur-bhuvah suvah iti va etas-tisro vyahritayah |
Tasamu ha sma-etam chaturthim Mahachamasyah pravedayate | Maha iti |
Tad-Brahma | Sa Atma | Angany-anya devatah |
Bhur-iti va ayam lokah | Bhuva ity-antariksham |
Suvar-ity-asau lokah | Maha ity-adityah || 4 ||

|| Ekadasho-anuvakah — Upadesha ||

Satyam vada | Dharmam chara | Svadhyayan-ma pramadah |
Acharyaya priyam dhanam-ahritya prajatantum ma vyavachchhetsih |
Satyan-na pramaditavyam | Dharman-na pramaditavyam |
Kushalan-na pramaditavyam | Bhutyai na pramaditavyam |
Svadhyaya-pravachanabhyam na pramaditavyam ||

Deva-pitri-karyabhyam na pramaditavyam |
Matri-devo bhava | Pitri-devo bhava |
Acharya-devo bhava | Atithi-devo bhava |
Yany-anavadyani karmani tani sevitavyani | No itarani |
Yany-asmakam su-charitani tani tvaya-upasyani | No itarani ||

Shraddhaya deyam | Ashraddhaya adeyam |
Shriya deyam | Hriya deyam | Bhiya deyam | Samvida deyam |
Esha adeshah | Esha upadeshah |
Esha Veda-Upanishat | Etad-anushasanam |
Evam-upasitavyam | Evam-u chaitat-upasyam || 11 ||

|| Dvadasho-anuvakah ||

Om Sham no Mitrah sham Varunah | Sham no bhavatv-Aryama |
Sham na Indro Brihaspatih | Sham no Vishnur-urukramah |
Namo Brahmane | Namaste Vayo |
Tvam-eva pratyaksham Brahma-asi | Tvam-eva pratyaksham Brahma vadishyami |
Ritam-avadisham | Satyam-avadisham |
Tan-mam-avit | Tad-vaktaram-avit |
Avin-mam | Avid-vaktaram |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih || 12 ||

|| Iti Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ मित्र हमारे लिए शुभ हों, वरुण शुभ हों, अर्यमा शुभ हों, इन्द्र-बृहस्पति शुभ हों, विष्णु उरुक्रम शुभ हों। ब्रह्म को नमस्कार, वायु को नमस्कार — तुम ही प्रत्यक्ष ब्रह्म हो। ऋत बोलूँगा, सत्य बोलूँगा। वह मेरी रक्षा करे, वक्ता की रक्षा करे। ॐ शान्तिः॥

शिक्षा की व्याख्या करेंगे — वर्ण, स्वर, मात्रा, बल, साम, सन्तान॥ १॥

ॐ वह (ब्रह्म) हम दोनों की रक्षा करे, हम दोनों को पोषित करे, हम दोनों सह-वीर्य करें, हमारा अधीत तेजस्वी हो, हम परस्पर द्वेष न करें। ॐ शान्तिः॥

संहिता उपनिषद् — पूर्वरूप, उत्तररूप, सन्धि, सन्धान। आध्यात्म में — आकाश सन्धि, प्राण सन्धान॥ २॥

भूः भुवः सुवः — ये तीन व्याहृतियाँ हैं। चौथी 'मह' — वही ब्रह्म है, वही आत्मा है। भूः यह लोक, भुवः अन्तरिक्ष, सुवः वह लोक, मह आदित्य॥ ४॥

सत्य बोलो। धर्म आचरण करो। स्वाध्याय में प्रमाद मत करो। गुरु को प्रिय धन देकर सन्तान परम्परा मत तोड़ो। सत्य, धर्म, कुशलता, समृद्धि, स्वाध्याय-प्रवचन, देव-पितृ कार्य — इनमें प्रमाद मत करो।

माता देवता हो। पिता देवता हो। आचार्य देवता हो। अतिथि देवता हो। जो निर्दोष कर्म हैं वे करो, अन्य नहीं। श्रद्धा से दो, अश्रद्धा से नहीं। श्री से, लज्जा से, भय से, ज्ञान से दो। यही आदेश है, यही उपदेश, यही वेदोपनिषद्, यही अनुशासन॥ ११॥
benefits: Contains the immortal teaching: Satyam Vada, Dharmam Chara
Foundation of Indian ethical and spiritual education
Saha Navavatu mantra for guru-shishya harmony
Ideal for daily recitation by students and spiritual seekers
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli (तैत्तिरीय उपनिषद् — शिक्षावल्ली) stands as a sacred prayer. Attributed to Taittiriya Upanishad (Krishna Yajurveda), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to saha Navavatu mantra for guru-shishya harmony, contain the immortal teaching: Satyam Vada, Dharmam Chara, and grant medha (intelligence), brahma-varchas, and fame. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima. At 12 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Contains Satyam Vada, Matridevo Bhava, and Saha Navavatu mantras.
alsoKnownAs: Sham No Mitrah Shanti Mantra Chapter
relatedSearches: Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli Sanskrit Hindi PDF download

Saha Navavatu Saha Nau Bhunaktu shanti mantra meaning
Matridevo Bhava Pitridevo Bhava Acharya Atithi shloka
Satyam Vada Dharmam Chara Tulsidas teaching
Taittiriya Upanishad three vallis Shiksha Brahmananda Bhrigu
Krishna Yajurveda Taittiriya Aranyaka Upanishad relation

Bhur Bhuvah Suvah Mahah Vyahriti meaning Taittiriya
Adi Shankaracharya Taittiriya Upanishad Bhashya commentary

seoFaqs.question: What is the Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiksha Valli (शिक्षावल्ली, literally 'chapter on phonetics / instruction') is the first of the three vallis (chapters) of the Taittiriya Upanishad, which belongs to the Taittiriya Aranyaka of the Krishna (Black) Yajurveda. It contains twelve anuvakas (sections) opening with the famous shanti mantra Sham No Mitrah Sham Varunah and closing with the Anushasanam — Vedic graduation address — that begins Satyam Vada, Dharmam Chara ('Speak the truth, walk the path of dharma'). The valli covers the science of correct Vedic recitation (varna, svara, matra, bala, sama, santana), meditations on the cosmic Vyahritis (Bhur, Bhuvah, Suvah, Mahah), the prayer for medha (intellect) and Brahma-varchas (spiritual lustre), and the immortal upadesha (instruction) to the graduating student. (Sources: Krishna Yajurveda; Taittiriya Aranyaka chapters 7-9; Adi Shankaracharya's Taittiriya Bhashya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the opening Sham No Mitrah shanti mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening shanti mantra invokes auspicious blessings (sham, peace) from the principal Vedic devatas before beginning Vedic study: Sham no Mitrah — may Mitra (god of friendship) be peaceful to us; Sham Varunah — may Varuna (cosmic order, waters); Sham no bhavatv-Aryama — may Aryama (god of pacts and travel); Sham na Indro Brihaspatih — may Indra (king of devas) and Brihaspati (deva-guru); Sham no Vishnur-urukramah — may Vishnu the wide-strider. Then the seeker addresses Brahma and Vayu directly: Tvam-eva pratyaksham Brahma-asi — 'You alone are the directly perceived Brahman' — and resolves Ritam vadishyami, Satyam vadishyami — 'I shall speak rita (cosmic order) and satya (truth)', praying that this Brahman protect both me (the student) and the teacher. It closes with the triple invocation of peace: Om Shantih Shantih Shantih. (Source: Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli, Anuvaka 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Saha Navavatu mantra and why is it recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Saha Navavatu (सह नाववतु) is the second peace-mantra of the Shiksha Valli (Anuvaka 2) and the most-recited guru-shishya prayer in the Vedic tradition: Om Saha nav-avatu — May (Brahman) protect us both; Saha nau bhunaktu — May He nourish us both; Saha viryam karavavhai — May we both work together with great vigour; Tejasvi nav-adhitam-astu — May our study be full of brilliance; Ma vidvishavahai — May we never hate one another; Om Shantih Shantih Shantih. It is chanted by the teacher (acharya) and the student (shishya) together at the start of every Vedic class, modern Sanskrit / yoga / Vedanta lesson, classical music or dance training, and any session of joint study — symbolising the sacred contract between guru and disciple. (Source: Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli, Anuvaka 2.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Matridevo Bhava, Pitridevo Bhava, Acharya-devo Bhava, Atithi-devo Bhava?
seoFaqs.answer: This four-fold adesha appears in the Anushasanam (graduation address) of the eleventh anuvaka and is one of the most quoted ethical teachings of the entire Sanatana Dharma: Matri-devo bhava — 'Be one to whom the mother is god'; Pitri-devo bhava — 'Be one to whom the father is god'; Acharya-devo bhava — 'Be one to whom the teacher is god'; Atithi-devo bhava — 'Be one to whom the guest is god' (the source of the Indian government's Incredible India tourism slogan 'Atithi Devo Bhava'). The acharya then instructs the graduating student: Yany-anavadyani karmani tani sevitavyani, no itarani — 'Only those actions that are blameless are to be followed, not others'; Shraddhaya deyam — 'Give with shraddha (faith)'; Hriya deyam — 'Give with humility'; Bhiya deyam — 'Give with respectful awe'. (Source: Taittiriya Upanishad Shiksha Valli, Anuvaka 11.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve anuvakas of the Shiksha Valli?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve anuvakas are: (1) Shanti mantra Sham no Mitrah; (2) Shiksha — phonetics: varna, svara, matra, bala, sama, santana, plus the Samhita Upanishad with Saha Navavatu; (3) Medha — prayer for intelligence, fame and Brahma-varchas; (4) the Medha-Sukta and Vyahriti meditation; (5) the four Vyahritis Bhur-Bhuvah-Suvah-Mahah identified with the worlds, devas, Vedas and pranas; (6) meditation on Brahman in the akasha within the heart; (7) cosmic correlations of pancha-bhautika and adhyatmika realities; (8) the Pranava Om as the foundation of all chanting; (9) the eleven topics worthy of study including dharma, satya, tapas, dama, shanti; (10) the Trishanku mantra and self-realisation declaration; (11) the famous Anushasanam (graduation upadesha — Satyam vada, Matri-devo bhava, etc.); (12) closing shanti mantra in past tense (Ritam-avadisham). (Source: Krishna Yajurveda Taittiriya Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Shiksha Valli be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiksha Valli is most powerfully recited at the daily Sandhya periods (sunrise, noon, sunset) as part of the Brahma-yajna (the daily duty of Vedic study prescribed for every dvija), on Thursday (Guruvar — the day of Brihaspati and the spiritual teacher), Guru Purnima (Ashadha Shukla Purnima — the supreme festival of the guru-shishya parampara), at the start and conclusion of any course of study (Vidyarambha and Samavartana), and during Upanayana ceremonies. The opening shanti mantra is chanted before beginning study; the Saha Navavatu before joint guru-shishya teaching sessions; and the entire eleventh anuvaka — the Anushasanam — is traditionally recited at Vedic graduation ceremonies and Brahmacharya completion. Daily householders, students preparing for examinations, teachers, scholars and seekers of jnana recite it for medha, brahma-varchas and ethical clarity. (Sources: Krishna Yajurveda Brahma-yajna vidhi; classical Vedic study tradition.)
